NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR SEP-10-2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON FEB-10-2012 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MTN TO AMEND COMPLT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LANE, DONNA HEARING SET FOR JUN-12-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DONNA LANE MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 19, 2012 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. ALL DISCOVERY DEADLINES ARE TRIGGERED OFF THE NEW TRIAL DATE. THE AUGUST 7, 2012 HEARING IS OFF CALENDAR. DEFENDANT MAY FILE A NEW MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. WITH THE TRIAL CONTINUANCE, DEFENDANT SUFFERS NO PREJUDICE BY THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION AND THUS THE STRONG POLICY OF LIBERALLY GRANTING AMENDMENTS CONTROLS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSES THAT SHE KNOWS AND IS SOCIAL FRIENDS WITH DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY ELIZABETH SALVESON. JUDGE MILLER PRACTICED LAW IN THE SAME FIRM AS MS SALVESON FROM DECEMBER 1981 - MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992 THROUGH 1995. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT JUDGE MILLER'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. JUDGE MILLER DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD REASONABLY HAVE A DOUBT THAT SHE WOULD BE ABLE TO BE IMPARTIAL. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Off Calendar Jan-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: PAMELA PITT, ESQ., 415.291.9251, FOR PLAINTIFF DONNA LANE.
ANDREW GSCHWIND, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

Judge Miller discloses that she knows and is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Judge Miller does not believe that a person aware of these facts would reasonably have a doubt that she would be able to be impartial.

Mtn To Amend Complt
Off Calendar Jun-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Pamela Pitt, Law Office, 415-291-9251, for plaintiff and moving party

No other Appearance. The Tentative Ruling is adopted as modified.
PLAINTIFF DONNA LANE Motion To Amend Complaint is Granted. The trial continued to November 19, 2012 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 206. All discovery deadlines are triggered off the new trial date. The August 7, 2012 hearing is off calendar. Defendant may file a new motion for summary judgment. With the trial continuance, defendant suffers no prejudice by the granting of the motion and thus the strong policy of liberally granting amendments controls. Order signed in open Court.
Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
